* **Influence \& Advocate:** Serve as a strong communicator who can advocate for ideas, drive alignment across global teams, and present data\-driven recommendations to leadership.

**Qualifications:**
-------------------

* **Full\-Stack Generalist:** Must perform as a highly efficient generalist with strong engineering skills across both front\-end (React) and server\-side (NodeJS, GCP, etc.). Fast learner and self\-starter.
* **Hacker Spirit:** A strong entrepreneurial mindset with experience in rapidly growing startups. Good judgment on when to hack and when to craft. You believe that speed is key and quality follows later.
* **Combat Mentality:** You dare to set ambitious goals and deadlines, working hard and smart. You resonate with the combat spirit to win (think F1 Movie) and treat challenges like a sport.
* **AI Native \& Product Sense:** Deeply understand creators, care passionately about end users, and possess strong product sense. Must be comfortable working at a frontier AI company.
* **Global Collaboration:** Open to having 1–2 evening meetings per week with Asia\-based senior engineers. *In\-person return\-to\-office in Palo Alto is required.*
* **Bonus:** Familiarity with growth marketing and distribution strategies (SEO, social, integrations), a background in building small, high\-leverage tools or side projects, or strong mobile skills (Swift/Kotlin).

**Tech Stack:**
---------------

* **Orchestration \& Compute:** Kubernetes (GKE), Google Cloud, Docker, Horizontal Pod Autoscaling (HPA).
* **Workflow Engine:** Temporal
* **Observability:** Datadog (APM, Custom Metrics, Alerting).
* **Coding Languages:** React, TypeScript, Python.
* **Data \& Storage:** Redis, Postgres.

**What We Offer!**
------------------

* Competitive Base Salary \+ Equity \+ Bonus


	+ Bonus Range: $15,000 \- $75,000
* Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans.
* Global team offsite.
* A challenging but exciting work environment with a strong culture of innovation and entrepreneurship.
* Opportunities for advancement and career growth.

**Location (Onsite):**
----------------------

* US (Onsite) \- Palo Alto

**EEO**


OpusClip is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate in hiring or any employment decision based on race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, genetic information, veteran status, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. OpusClip considers qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Opus Clip is also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures.

title: Director, Growth Engineering
description:
Scribd, Inc. is on a mission to advance human understanding. Our four products — Scribd, Slideshare, Everand, and Fable — help billions of people across the globe move beyond access and into insight, application, and expertise.

**Culture at Scribd, Inc.**
---------------------------


We support a culture where our employees can be real and be bold; where we debate and commit as we embrace plot twists; and where every employee is empowered to take action as we prioritize the customer.


We believe the best work happens when individual flexibility is balanced with meaningful community connection. Scribd Flex empowers employees to choose the workstyle and location that support their best performance, while committing to intentional in\-person moments that strengthen collaboration and culture. Occasional in\-person attendance is required for all Scribd, Inc. employees, regardless of location.


So what are we looking for in new team members? At Scribd, Inc., we hire for “GRIT.” Traditionally defined as the intersection of passion and perseverance toward long\-term goals, GRIT reflects the mindset we expect from every employee. For us, it also serves as a practical framework for how we work: setting and achieving Goals, delivering Results within your role, contributing Innovative ideas and solutions, and strengthening the broader Team through collaboration and attitude.


This posting reflects an approved, open position within the organization.

**About the Role**
------------------


We're looking for a Director of Engineering to own the engineering vision, strategy, and execution for Scribd's growth organization. You will build modern growth and MarTech systems for a global audience of \+200M monthly visitors and \+2M paying subscribers across multiple brands.


You'll lead an organization of approximately 25 engineers across three teams, directly managing a growth platform team while providing strategic and organizational leadership across the full group. You will operate at two altitudes simultaneously: hands\-on with your platform team while setting direction, raising the bar, and removing obstacles across the broader org.


This is a high\-ownership leadership role for a Director who is both people\-first and visionary: someone who is passionate about growth engineering and maximizing the potential of an already high\-performing team.


This role requires deep technical expertise in designing and shipping growth systems. You’ll partner closely with leaders in Product, Performance Marketing, Partnerships, Data Platform, and Analytics to build reliable foundations, and to translate growth strategy into high\-leverage technical capabilities.

**What You’ll Do**
------------------

* **Define the growth engineering strategy.** Audit the current state of our growth tooling, data pipelines, experimentation infrastructure, lifecycle messaging, analytics, and attribution. Develop a technical roadmap in close partnership with product leadership.
* **Build and lead the growth platform team.** Take direct management of a 5\-person team focused on our growth platform. Establish engineering practices and define the team charter. Create team identity and build the team to full strength.
* **Lead a high\-performing growth engineering organization.** Provide organizational leadership across all three growth engineering teams. Assess team boundaries, build alignment on shared engineering standards, and strengthen the culture of experimentation rigor. Maintain engineering quality while strategically balancing speed and technical debt.
* **Drive agentic engineering adoption.** Lead the adoption of agentic engineering practices within the growth organization. Identify how AI can accelerate delivery while maintaining high quality.
* **Be a strategic partner.** Serve as the engineering counterpart to your peer product leader in the growth pillar. Build strong relationships across Product, Design, Marketing, Analytics, Data Science, and Partnerships. Create visibility into growth engineering's impact through business reviews, roadmap updates, and clear reporting that connects technical work to business outcomes.
* **Raise the bar across Scribd engineering.** Contribute to the broader engineering organization through leadership forums, architecture reviews, hiring, and engineering\-wide initiatives. Model engineering excellence and be a collaborative peer leader among Scribd's engineering directors.

**What We’re Looking For**
--------------------------

* **Growth platform and martech depth.** You've directly built or led the build\-out of growth systems at a consumer subscription or marketplace business. You understand the modern stack and vendor landscape deeply.
* **Technical depth.** You can make sound design decisions about web frontend, backend, and data\-intensive systems, and you are comfortable with warehouse\-centric architectures. You can lead teams through ambiguity and tradeoffs.
* **Proven engineering leadership.** 10\+ years of engineering experience with 4\+ years leading multiple engineering teams, including managing managers. Track record of hiring and developing strong talent. You build trust, invest in people, and hold teams to a high bar.

title: GTM Engineer
description:
CaptivateIQ is transforming the way companies plan, manage, and optimize sales performance. We started by revolutionizing incentive compensation management, and now we're expanding our platform to solve broader sales planning challenges. Recognized by industry analysts like Forrester and G2 and backed by top\-tier investors, including Sequoia, ICONIQ, Accel, and Sapphire Ventures, we empower high\-growth companies like Netflix, Figma, and Stripe with the flexibility and insights needed to drive revenue performance.  

Join a talented, fast\-growing team committed to solving some of the most complex and impactful problems in sales performance management. **About the Role**
This role exists to make our go\-to\-market engine faster, cleaner, and more scalable by turning manual GTM work into reliable systems and AI\-powered workflows. The goal is simple: reduce the time teams spend on busywork, improve targeting and follow\-through, and give leadership trustworthy visibility into what’s working across campaigns, channels, and the full customer lifecycle.  

You’ll operate as a builder at the center of our GTM stack, partnering across RevOps, Sales, Marketing, and CS to spot bottlenecks and design practical solutions. Sometimes that’s an AI copilot that helps reps act on signals. Sometimes it’s a data pipeline that finally makes attribution and lifecycle reporting consistent. Sometimes it’s automation that quietly removes 20 steps from a process no one has time to fix. **Job Location**
The candidate selected for this opportunity must reside near one of the following locations: **Hybrid (in\-office 3 days per week)*** Austin, TX

 **Remote*** Raleigh, NC
* Nashville, TN
* Toronto, Canada


### **Responsibilities**

* Map GTM bottlenecks: Partner with RevOps, Sales, Marketing, and CS to identify where AI can reduce manual effort, increase speed, or improve targeting.
* Create AI\-powered automations, copilots, and agents to support GTM Teams.
* Design and maintain scalable data pipelines that unify data across Hubspot, Salesforce, Outreach, Gong, ChurnZero, etc).
* Implement systems that measure campaign, channel, and program effectiveness.
* Identify and design automated workflows to support our team’s internal operations.
* Innovate and experiment. Pilot new tools, integrations, and AI workflows.

### **Requirements**

* 5\+ years as a Marketing Analyst or technical role in Marketing Operations/RevOps
* You are comfortable with writing code (Python/SQL) and working with APIs. Bonus if you have a background in data engineering or ETL tools.
* Deep knowledge of Salesforce Architecture
* Fluency in other GTM tools (Clay, HubSpot/Marketo, Outreach / Apollo, Gong, Nooks Dialier/Orum, 6sense, Usergems, etc), APIs, and automation platforms (Zapier, Workato).
* Curious, scrappy builder with a love for experimentation who thrives in ambiguity, enjoys automating manual work, and owns projects end\-to\-end.
* The ability to translate business problems into elegant, maintainable system solutions
* Strong attention to detail and high standards for data quality and process consistency
* Proven ability to design and scale processes across global marketing teams.
* Self\-starter who thrives in fast\-paced, collaborative, and remote environments.

### **Benefits**

* Comprehensive Healthcare: 100% coverage for medical, dental, and vision for all FTEs, with roughly 75% coverage for dependents.
* Flexible Time Off: Flexible vacation days plus quarterly mental health days to ensure you have the space to recharge.
* Annual Stipends: Dedicated funds for your professional development and caretaking needs.
* Work Anniversary Bonuses: Annual bonuses to celebrate your milestones and contributions to the CaptivateIQ team that grow as your tenure does.
* Retirement Savings (US\-Only): A 401(k) plan to help you invest in and secure your future.
* Premium Tools: The latest Apple hardware to empower you to do your best work.
* Inclusive Community: Active Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) that celebrate shared identities and support our DEI goals by fostering an environment where diverse talent thrives.

### **Notice for Prospective Candidates**

* Only emails from @captivateiq.com should be trusted.


We are aware of active recruitment scams using the CaptivateIQ name, in which individuals pose as our recruiters and post fake remote job openings and make fake job offers on the Internet. Please note, we will never do the following:* Attempt to correspond with a candidate using a free web\-based account, such as an email address that ends in @gmail.com, @yahoo.com, @hotmail.com, etc.
* Make an offer of employment without conducting multiple rounds of interviews face\-to\-face using secure video\-conferencing technology.
* Ask candidates to cash checks to buy equipment on behalf of CaptivateIQ.
* Ask candidates to make a payment in order to be considered for a position.
* Make early requests for candidates' personal information such as date of birth, passport details, credit card numbers, bank details and social security number, etc.
* Please note that we’ll only ask for more sensitive personal information in connection with background checks after an offer is made.


The OTE range represents the minimum and maximum for this position across North America. For candidates in Canada, the range is $155,000–$185,000 CAD. The OTE offered for this position will depend on numerous factors, including individual proficiency, anticipated performance, and the location of the selected candidate. Our OTE is just one component of CaptivateIQ's competitive total rewards package.*CaptivateIQ participates in E\-Verify, web\-based system that allows enrolled employers to confirm the eligibility of their employees to work in the United States*

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Director, Growth Engineering
description:
Scribd, Inc. is on a mission to advance human understanding. Our four products — Scribd, Slideshare, Everand, and Fable — help billions of people across the globe move beyond access and into insight, application, and expertise.

**Culture at Scribd, Inc.**
---------------------------


We support a culture where our employees can be real and be bold; where we debate and commit as we embrace plot twists; and where every employee is empowered to take action as we prioritize the customer.


We believe the best work happens when individual flexibility is balanced with meaningful community connection. Scribd Flex empowers employees to choose the workstyle and location that support their best performance, while committing to intentional in\-person moments that strengthen collaboration and culture. Occasional in\-person attendance is required for all Scribd, Inc. employees, regardless of location.


So what are we looking for in new team members? At Scribd, Inc., we hire for “GRIT.” Traditionally defined as the intersection of passion and perseverance toward long\-term goals, GRIT reflects the mindset we expect from every employee. For us, it also serves as a practical framework for how we work: setting and achieving Goals, delivering Results within your role, contributing Innovative ideas and solutions, and strengthening the broader Team through collaboration and attitude.


This posting reflects an approved, open position within the organization.

**About the Role**
------------------


We're looking for a Director of Engineering to own the engineering vision, strategy, and execution for Scribd's growth organization. You will build modern growth and MarTech systems for a global audience of \+200M monthly visitors and \+2M paying subscribers across multiple brands.


You'll lead an organization of approximately 25 engineers across three teams, directly managing a growth platform team while providing strategic and organizational leadership across the full group. You will operate at two altitudes simultaneously: hands\-on with your platform team while setting direction, raising the bar, and removing obstacles across the broader org.


This is a high\-ownership leadership role for a Director who is both people\-first and visionary: someone who is passionate about growth engineering and maximizing the potential of an already high\-performing team.


This role requires deep technical expertise in designing and shipping growth systems. You’ll partner closely with leaders in Product, Performance Marketing, Partnerships, Data Platform, and Analytics to build reliable foundations, and to translate growth strategy into high\-leverage technical capabilities.

**What You’ll Do**
------------------

* **Define the growth engineering strategy.** Audit the current state of our growth tooling, data pipelines, experimentation infrastructure, lifecycle messaging, analytics, and attribution. Develop a technical roadmap in close partnership with product leadership.
* **Build and lead the growth platform team.** Take direct management of a 5\-person team focused on our growth platform. Establish engineering practices and define the team charter. Create team identity and build the team to full strength.
* **Lead a high\-performing growth engineering organization.** Provide organizational leadership across all three growth engineering teams. Assess team boundaries, build alignment on shared engineering standards, and strengthen the culture of experimentation rigor. Maintain engineering quality while strategically balancing speed and technical debt.
* **Drive agentic engineering adoption.** Lead the adoption of agentic engineering practices within the growth organization. Identify how AI can accelerate delivery while maintaining high quality.
* **Be a strategic partner.** Serve as the engineering counterpart to your peer product leader in the growth pillar. Build strong relationships across Product, Design, Marketing, Analytics, Data Science, and Partnerships. Create visibility into growth engineering's impact through business reviews, roadmap updates, and clear reporting that connects technical work to business outcomes.
* **Raise the bar across Scribd engineering.** Contribute to the broader engineering organization through leadership forums, architecture reviews, hiring, and engineering\-wide initiatives. Model engineering excellence and be a collaborative peer leader among Scribd's engineering directors.

**What We’re Looking For**
--------------------------

* **Growth platform and martech depth.** You've directly built or led the build\-out of growth systems at a consumer subscription or marketplace business. You understand the modern stack and vendor landscape deeply.
* **Technical depth.** You can make sound design decisions about web frontend, backend, and data\-intensive systems, and you are comfortable with warehouse\-centric architectures. You can lead teams through ambiguity and tradeoffs.
* **Proven engineering leadership.** 10\+ years of engineering experience with 4\+ years leading multiple engineering teams, including managing managers. Track record of hiring and developing strong talent. You build trust, invest in people, and hold teams to a high bar.
* **Cross\-functional partnership.** You thrive in collaborative environments. You can code\-switch between technical and business audiences. You build alignment through influence, not authority, and you're as credible in an executive review as you are in an architecture discussion.
* **Bias toward action.** You move with urgency. You're comfortable making decisions with incomplete information and course\-correcting as you learn. You thrive in ambiguity and can build structure where none exists.

**Why This Role, Why Now?**
---------------------------


We are entering the next phase of growth engineering at Scribd, systematically investing in a holistic platform and practices that drive sustained business impact at scale: from paid, partnerships, plans \& pricing, to onboarding and retention.


If you're looking for a role where you can define the architecture (not just manage the backlog), ship systems that directly move conversion and revenue, build a team and culture, and do it all alongside sharp, collaborative people who care – we'd love to talk!


\-


At Scribd, Inc., your base pay is one part of your total compensation package and is determined within a range. Our pay ranges are based on the local cost of labor benchmarks for each specific role, level, and geographic location. San Francisco is our highest geographic market in the United States.


In the state of California, the reasonably expected salary range is between $196,500 \[minimum salary in our lowest geographic market within California] to $318,000 \[maximum salary in our highest geographic market within California].


In the United States, outside of California, the reasonably expected salary range is between $162,000 \[minimum salary in our lowest US geographic market outside of California] to $302,000 \[maximum salary in our highest US geographic market outside of California].


In Canada, the reasonably expected salary range is between $205,500 CAD\[minimum salary in our lowest geographic market] to $282,000 CAD\[maximum salary in our highest geographic market].


We carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining compensation, including but not limited to experience; job\-related skill sets; relevant education or training; and other business and organizational needs. Create visibility into growth engineering's impact through business reviews, roadmap updates, and clear reporting that connects technical work to business outcomes.
* **Raise the bar across Scribd engineering.** Contribute to the broader engineering organization through leadership forums, architecture reviews, hiring, and engineering\-wide initiatives. Model engineering excellence and be a collaborative peer leader among Scribd's engineering directors.

**What We’re Looking For**
--------------------------

* **Growth platform and martech depth.** You've directly built or led the build\-out of growth systems at a consumer subscription or marketplace business. You understand the modern stack and vendor landscape deeply.
* **Technical depth.** You can make sound design decisions about web frontend, backend, and data\-intensive systems, and you are comfortable with warehouse\-centric architectures. You can lead teams through ambiguity and tradeoffs.
* **Proven engineering leadership.** 10\+ years of engineering experience with 4\+ years leading multiple engineering teams, including managing managers. Track record of hiring and developing strong talent. You build trust, invest in people, and hold teams to a high bar.
* **Cross\-functional partnership.** You thrive in collaborative environments. You can code\-switch between technical and business audiences. You build alignment through influence, not authority, and you're as credible in an executive review as you are in an architecture discussion.
* **Bias toward action.** You move with urgency. You're comfortable making decisions with incomplete information and course\-correcting as you learn. You thrive in ambiguity and can build structure where none exists.

**Why This Role, Why Now?**
---------------------------


We are entering the next phase of growth engineering at Scribd, systematically investing in a holistic platform and practices that drive sustained business impact at scale: from paid, partnerships, plans \& pricing, to onboarding and retention.


If you're looking for a role where you can define the architecture (not just manage the backlog), ship systems that directly move conversion and revenue, build a team and culture, and do it all alongside sharp, collaborative people who care – we'd love to talk!


\-

title: GTM Engineer - Demand Generation
description:
HackerRank helps companies like NVIDIA, Amazon, and Microsoft hire and upskill the next generation of developers based on skills, not pedigree. Our platform is trusted by over 2,500 of the world's most innovative companies to build strong engineering teams ready for what's next.  

  

Software has entered an era where humans and AI build side by side. As this shift accelerates, the definition of strong technical talent is changing. We give companies better ways to identify and invest in next\-generation skills.  

  

People at HackerRank care deeply about the impact of their work and sweat the small details so our customers can be wildly successful with products they genuinely love to use. We move with urgency and believe great outcomes come from high standards

#### **About the role**



The Growth Marketing team is the engine behind pipeline acceleration at HackerRank. We operate at the intersection of creativity, analytics, and automation, managing paid channels, orchestrating ABM plays, and optimizing every touchpoint that turns interest into opportunity. As the team responsible for bringing in qualified leads and nurturing them across the funnel, we directly impact revenue and go\-to\-market success.



As our Demand\-focused GTM Engineer, you'll be the driving force that turns marketing strategy into pipeline. You will own the day\-to\-day execution of multi\-channel demand programs including paid social, ABM plays, email nurture, and direct mail while managing budgets, creative assets, and stakeholders. You'll analyze campaign data, optimize performance, and scale efforts to maximize impact. If juggling timelines, audience lists, and bid strategies is your wheelhouse, this is your chance to help HackerRank hit the next level of growth.


#### **What you'll do**


* Execute and optimize full\-funnel demand generation programs across paid media and owned channels.
* Lead the day\-to\-day management of paid media, audience targeting, creative rotation, pacing, and bid strategies.
* Drive ABM campaigns (1:Many, 1:Few, and 1:1\), collaborating with SDRs, design, and content to engage key accounts.
* Run diagnostics across GTM funnel systems, report on campaign performance, and implement A/B and multivariate tests.
* Streamline campaign operations with documented playbooks and automation to reduce manual effort and increase speed.

#### **Who you are**


* Connects strategy with hands‑on execution across channels and tools.
* Passionate about data, testing, and finding signal in the noise.
* Proven track record driving pipeline growth through high‑performing ABM programs.
* You bring 5\+ years in B2B demand generation or growth marketing (agency or SaaS).
* Hands‑on with LinkedIn Ads and at least one more paid platform (Meta, Reddit, etc.).
* Experience running 1:Many, 1:Few, and 1:1 ABM campaigns with measurable results.
* Fluent with CRM and marketing automation (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot).
* Strong in reporting, attribution modeling, and funnel diagnostics (pivot tables, UTM tracking, etc.).
* Skilled at conversion tracking and syncing audience segments (Tag Manager, Analytics events, etc.).
* Familiar with low‑code and automation tools such as Clay, Make, N8N, or Zapier.

#### **Even better if you have**


* Experience with APIs, webhooks, dashboarding, or ETL.
* You bring light creative skills with tools like Figma or Canva.
* Have copywriting chops to iterate messaging and improve campaign performance.

#### **You will thrive in this role if**


* You love turning strategy into scalable, high\-performing campaigns.
* Experimentation excites you, you test, learn, and double down on what works.
* You thrive at the intersection of data, creativity, and collaboration.
* Driving measurable growth while keeping customer experience front and center motivates you.

#### **Compensation**



This role is eligible for incentive\-based pay, with an annual total potential earnings range of $120,000 – $150,000, including base salary and incentive\-based compensation (based on quota attainment).


#### **Want to stand out?**



Send your name, Linkedin, and message in JSON to this endpoint:



 https://hook.eu2\.make.com/swxvg5yiu1c5fmiag2ccmfs3fu3em92k

**Want to learn more about HackerRank?** Check out HackerRank.com to explore our products, solutions and resources, and dive into our story and mission here.



HackerRank is a proud equal employment opportunity and affirmative action employer. We provide equal opportunity to everyone for employment based on individual performance and qualification. title: Director, GTM Automation & Lifecycle Systems
description:
**Director, Automation and Lifecycle Systems**

*Full\-time*

*Remote within the U.S.*

*Exempt*

  

At Ignite Visibility, we’re on a mission to revolutionize national\-to\-local digital marketing. As an award\-winning, fast\-growing agency, we pride ourselves on innovation, collaboration, and excellence. We’re looking for a disciplined, results\-driven leader to build the systems that power how our marketing converts interest into revenue.


We need a visionary who can influence stakeholders and deliver measurable results at scale—all while managing a high\-performing automation ecosystem. You will not simply manage marketing automation; you will design the technical architecture of our revenue engine.

**The Role**


This position sits at the intersection of lifecycle marketing, marketing automation, data architecture, and AI\-driven automation. You will move beyond traditional nurture campaigns to build a modern growth engine that utilizes integrations and AI\-powered workflows to directly improve marketing efficiency and revenue performance.

**What You’ll Do**

* Own Lifecycle Growth Systems: Design and operate the architecture driving engagement across lead capture, multi\-channel nurture, product adoption, and retention.
* Scale Marketing Automation: Architect and implement Salesforce Marketing Cloud (SFMC) and Salesforce Data Cloud.
* Architect Data Flows: Design schemas for lifecycle marketing, manage customer identity resolution, and ensure multi\-touch attribution and revenue feedback loops function properly.
* Build AI\-Driven Workflows: Lead the development of automation\-first systems, including predictive engagement models and intelligent lead routing.
* Technical Integration: Build and manage API\-based integrations (e.g., Zapier) across the GTM stack to trigger automation.

**What We’re Looking For**

* Experience: 7\+ years in lifecycle marketing, automation, or RevOps.
* Technical Expertise: Recent, hands\-on experience with Salesforce Marketing Cloud data platforms and a strong Salesforce CRM background.
* Integration Skills: Proficiency in API integrations and middleware like Zapier to connect disparate GTM tools.
* Strategic Execution: Ability to design multi\-touch programs and scalable architecture that connects marketing data to revenue outcomes.
* Data\-Driven Mindset: A deeply analytical professional who uses data schemas and event tracking to ensure every lifecycle program is measurable and tied to ROI.
* Soft Skills: We value a curious, autonomous self\-starter with an "automation\-first" mindset and a passion for AI\-driven growth.

**Success in 6 Months**

* Build a scalable lifecycle automation framework and implement it in Marketing Cloud/Data Cloud.
* Implement integrated lifecycle data flows and multi\-touch models across our GTM stack.
* Deploy automated programs across key funnel stages to improve marketing efficiency.
* Strengthen the connection between marketing activity and revenue outcomes.

  

Ready to build the infrastructure that allows marketing to operate as a data\-driven revenue engine?


Would you like me to generate a technical assessment test to evaluate candidates on their Salesforce Data Cloud and Zapier integration skills? Then apply today for immediate consideration.

title: Go-To-Market Engineer
description:
**About DoorLoop**

**﻿**DoorLoop is property management software built for speed and the smart choice for people who take growth seriously. With offices in Miami, New York City, and Tel Aviv, we’re a global company helping property owners and managers move faster, scale smarter, and get real support, real fast.


We’re proudly People First. That’s why we’re a Certified Great Place to Work, recognized by Forbes as one of America’s Best Startup Employers in both 2024 and 2025, and earn top ratings on Glassdoor.

**Mission**


DoorLoop is hiring a GTM Engineer to rebuild and scale our GTM systems and architecture as we move upmarket and support enterprise GTM execution. This role will play a critical part in transforming a legacy Salesforce environment—originally designed for a single funnel—into a flexible, multi\-funnel platform that supports complex routing, trusted attribution, scalable automation, and AI\-powered GTM workflows across Marketing, Sales, and RevOps.


This is a hands\-on builder role for someone who enjoys solving complex GTM problems, reducing manual work, and designing durable, intelligent architecture that enables speed today without creating tech debt tomorrow to bolster revenue generation.

**Responsibilities**

* Own Salesforce architecture and GTM system design, including objects, fields, relationships, flows, validation logic, routing, and integrations.
* Rebuild and simplify a heavily customized legacy Salesforce environment to support enterprise GTM execution and long\-term scale.
* Design, build, and maintain GTM automations and workflows, including AI\-powered workflows and decisioning, to reduce manual effort and improve reliability.
* Implement flexible, segment\-aware routing frameworks, incorporating deterministic logic and AI\-assisted decisioning where appropriate.
* Design and implement GTM system changes using an experimentation mindset, including clear hypotheses, safe rollout strategies, validation plans, and pre\-/post\-change measurement.
* Partner closely with Marketing and RevOps to support lifecycle management, lead and account scoring, attribution, and segmentation infrastructure.
* Support account\-based GTM strategies through account hierarchies, enrichment, scoring, and targeting logic.
* Collaborate with Data Engineering and Analytics to ensure Salesforce architecture cleanly feeds Snowflake and Tableau for downstream reporting.
* Leverage webhooks, workflow orchestration tools (e.g., n8n), AI services, and code\-based logic to extend GTM automation beyond Salesforce.
* Integrate and leverage web analytics and product data (e.g., PostHog) to support lifecycle logic, attribution, routing, and GTM experimentation.
* Ensure GTM system reliability and data quality, proactively identifying and resolving issues before they impact the business.
* Document system architecture, workflows, automation decisions, and experiment outcomes, including AI usage and guardrails, to ensure maintainability and shared understanding.
* Participate in RevOps intake and prioritization, balancing strategic system rebuilds with ongoing GTM needs.

  


Requirements:  

  

**Must\-Have Experience \& Attributes**

* 3–6\+ years of experience in GTM Engineering, Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, or similar roles at a high\-growth SaaS company.
* Deep hands\-on experience with Salesforce architecture, including complex object relationships and automation (Flows, validation rules).
* Strong systems\-thinking mindset with experience rebuilding or refactoring legacy GTM systems.
* Experience integrating Salesforce with marketing automation platforms (e.g., HubSpot), web analytics platforms (e.g., PostHog), and other GTM tooling.
* Strong understanding of GTM funnels, including routing, lifecycle stages, scoring, and attribution.
* Experience designing or implementing AI\-powered workflows, decisioning, or automation within GTM systems.
* Comfort working with webhooks, workflow automation tools, AI services, and code\-like logic, even if you are not a traditional software engineer.
* Demonstrated ability to balance precision and speed — measuring twice and building systems that last.
* Comfortable pushing back and saying “no” when a request introduces unnecessary complexity or long\-term tech debt.
* Strong cross\-functional collaboration skills and clear communication with technical and non\-technical stakeholders.
* High ownership mentality with a strong bar for reliability, quality, and measurable impact.

**Nice\-to\-Have**

* Apex or programmatic Salesforce experience for edge cases.
* Experience with workflow orchestration tools such as n8n.
* Experience operationalizing AI models or agents within production GTM workflows.
* Familiarity with enrichment and ABM tooling (ZoomInfo, Clearbit, Clay).
* Understanding of how CRM and product data feed data warehouses and BI tools.


\*\*\*This is not a data engineering role

**Preferred Traits**

* Builder mindset: pragmatic, curious, and solutions\-oriented.
* Strong attention to detail without losing sight of long\-term scalability.
* Comfortable operating in ambiguity and defining better system patterns.
* Motivated by leverage — you want your work to make GTM teams faster and more effective.

  


Benefits:
  

* **Unlimited paid time off:** take unlimited personal, sick, and vacation days.
* **401(k):** plan for retirement with 4% matching and instant vesting.
* **Medical, dental, and vision insurance:** we offer full medical coverage for employees through United Healthcare and full dental and vision coverage through Guardian, with 25% coverage for dependents across all plans.
* **Life insurance:** $100,000 policy fully covered by DoorLoop.
* **Disability insurance:** short\- and long\-term disability insurance fully covered by DoorLoop.
* **Paid parental leave:** paid maternity and paternity leave for birth and adoption.
* **Best\-in\-class equipment:** Get a company laptop and all the top\-tier tools to set you up for success.

  

Compensation Range:  

  

The salary range for this position is $140,000\-$180,000 annually. Actual compensation will vary based on a candidate's qualifications, experience, and other relevant factors.

  

How we use AI:  

  

We may use AI tools to help review resumes and applications, with human oversight at all times. Please review our privacy policy.

title: GTM Engineer
description:
At SolarWinds, we're a people\-first company. Our purpose is to enrich the lives of the people we serve—including our employees, customers, shareholders, partners, and communities. Join us in our mission to help customers accelerate business transformation with simple, powerful, and secure solutions.



The ideal candidate thrives in an innovative, fast\-paced environment and is collaborative, accountable, ready, and empathetic. We're looking for individuals who believe they can accomplish more as a team and create lasting growth for themselves and others. We hire based on attitude, competency, and commitment. Solarians are ready to advance our world\-class solutions in a fast\-paced environment and accept the challenge to lead with purpose. If you're looking to build your career with an exceptional team, you've come to the right place. Join SolarWinds and grow with us!

**Overview**



SolarWinds is undergoing a significant transformation toward our cloud future. This pivotal moment requires a new team of expert technology strategists to deliver against our strategic vision. This role focuses on scaling AI\-powered agents and automation tools to support the demand generation campaigns for our Observability, Service Management, and Database subscription portfolio.



Team \& Reporting: This position is a key part of the GTM Technology team within Marketing Strategy \& Operations. The GTM Tech team supports both Marketing and Sales by providing tools, enablement, and insights to manage the overall lead flow across inbound and outbound activities.


**Core Responsibilities**


* The GTM Engineer will be responsible for the full lifecycle of AI and automation solutions, from design to governance.
* AI Engineering \& Automation
* Design, develop, and deploy AI agents and machine learning orchestration patterns (e.g., prompts, chains, context packs) for GTM functions.
* Build and maintain complex Zapier Canvas automations and high\-impact GTM integrations.
* Develop retrieval integrations (e.g., Glean/Genie, transcripts, product telemetry) to ensure agents are grounded with accurate, real\-time business context.
* Implement and manage Human\-in\-the\-Loop (HITL) approval workflows for critical processes.
* Build reusable automation modules, templates, and workflow standards for enterprise\-wide adoption.


**Operations, Governance, and Reliability**


* Establish scalable governance frameworks and processes for AI agent deployment, behavior management, and compliance with ethical and security guidelines.
* Monitor system health, error rates, and uptime, ensuring integration reliability across the GTM stack (Salesforce, Marketo, Outreach, Qualified, 6sense, Slack, Google).
* Define, track, and continuously optimize key performance indicators (KPIs) for agent task accuracy, resolution speed, and measurable business impact (ROI).


**GTM Strategy \& Enablement**


* Translate sophisticated marketing and sales programs into technical specifications, with a strong emphasis on programmatic execution and evergreen email nurture design.
* Serve as the internal AI evangelist, developing documentation, knowledge bases, and training playbooks to empower cross\-functional teams in adopting agentic solutions.
* Work closely with stakeholders in Demand Generation, IT, Security, and Sales to align AI deployments with broader business priorities.


**Required Technical Qualifications**


* Experience: 3–5 years in B2B marketing, Revenue Operations, or Sales Strategy, with a deep understanding of lead funnels, ABM principles, and demand generation metrics.
* AI/LLM Proficiency: Proven experience with AI prompt engineering, understanding of agent alignment, and failure mode management in AI systems.
* Data \& Systems Literacy: Comfort with structured and unstructured datasets, strong preference for SQL proficiency, and the ability to independently analyze agent performance data.
* Platform Expertise: Proficiency in a range of GTM and automation tools, including:
* CRM (Salesforce/SFDC)
* Marketing Automation Platforms
* Automation Platforms (Zapier, Make, Retool, Relevance AI)
* Familiarity with agent platforms like Glean Agents, Google Agentspace, or similar ecosystems.


**Preferred but Not Required**


* Foundational knowledge in Python or JavaScript, or "vibe\-coding" aptitude, for managing tool interactions and API\-first platforms.
* Experience with LLMs, agent architectures, or agent orchestration frameworks (like LangChain or CrewAI).
* Familiarity with REST APIs and integrating third\-party systems.
* Background in no\-code/low\-code platforms.
* Experience implementing MCP frameworks between GTM tools.

  

SolarWinds is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. SolarWinds will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, disability, veteran status or any other characteristic protected by law.



All applications are treated in accordance with the SolarWinds Privacy Notice: https://www.solarwinds.com/applicant\-privacy\-notice

title: GTM Engineer
description:
**About Sim**
-------------


Sim is building the leading platform for AI agents—enabling developers and enterprises to visually create, launch, and manage high\-performing agents that automate the world’s workflows. Our mission is simple: agents will run the world, and Sim is the primary means by which that happens. We’re backed by world\-class investors including Standard Capital, Paul Graham, Perplexity, SV Angel, and Y Combinator, and we’ve been open source from day one.


We're a small, fast\-moving team based in San Francisco, building together in person every day. 70,000\+ developers use Sim; your work ships fast and impacts real users at scale. We believe in ownership, excellence, and ambitious builders shaping the future from the ground up.

**About the Role**
------------------


This is a foundational role: Sim is hiring our first GTM Engineer to build the company’s go\-to\-market and RevOps infrastructure from the ground up. You won’t just optimize existing workflows—you'll architect them. You’ll partner directly with leadership to define, implement, and scale our GTM systems, automations, and data flows. If you want to operate as a technical founder for our GTM function and ship quickly with massive ownership, this role is for you.


You'll work full\-time, in\-person, out of our San Francisco office.

**What You'll Do:**
-------------------

* **Build from zero:** Create Sim’s GTM infrastructure from scratch—integrating best\-in\-class tools like Salesforce, Clay, Gong, enrichment APIs, and more into a seamless automated engine
* **Invent internal automation:** Design and build AI agents and custom workflows to automate account research, minimize manual tasks, and personalize outreach at scale
* **Engineer data flows:** Architect the middleware and webhooks that ensure data integrity and sync between Sim’s product, CRM, and internal systems
* **Programmatic GTM:** Translate top\-level GTM strategy into automated workflows, custom internal tools, and data\-driven outbound campaigns
* **Found and scale:** Set the technical standard and shape the DNA of Sim’s GTM/Revenue Operations function for years to come

**What We’re Looking For**
--------------------------

* 3\+ years of experience in GTM Engineering, technical RevOps, or similar technical roles
* Proficiency writing code (Python, JavaScript, or SQL) and familiarity working with APIs
* Track record of integrating, automating, and scaling SaaS and AI tool stacks
* Strong understanding of sales funnels, GTM business logic, and operations
* Experience with modern GTM tools (Clay, Claude Code, Parallel, Unify, Sim, scraping/indexing the web, etc.)
* High bias toward action and "shipping v1 today"—you thrive in ambiguity and fast\-paced teams
* Deep ownership mentality; you want end\-to\-end responsibility for Sim’s GTM architecture and future team

**Why Sim**
-----------

* **Foundational impact:** You’ll build and define Sim’s GTM infrastructure and shape how agents are sold, deployed, and supported
* **Immediate scale:** Your work ships to 70,000\+ developers and customers right now
* **World\-class backers:** Series A led by Standard Capital, with Paul Graham, Perplexity, SV Angel, and Y Combinator
* **Open source culture:** 26,000\+ GitHub stars and a passionate developer community
* **Competitive compensation:** Competitive pay \+ meaningful equity
* **Full benefits:** Comprehensive health insurance and relocation support
* **Visa sponsorship available**

**Equal Opportunity**
---------------------


Sim is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all team members. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.


Compensation Range: $120K \- $200K
